label: Candy cane club
desc: A blunt weapon made of hard candy—sweet and mean. A common starter weapon in the holiday dungeon.

label: Silver star dagger
desc: A six-point star etched on the blade; light and sneaky. Grants a touch of dexterity.

label: Holiday warhammer
desc: A gold-foil wrapped head that hits with real weight. Needs a strong arm.

label: Aurora greatsword
desc: The blade catches rainbow glints; some say it hums on long winter nights. Heavy and deadly.

label: Midnight carol blade
desc: Runes of silent hymns along the spine; every swing feels like a distant choir.

label: Wreath buckler
desc: A small shield ringed with holly; smells faintly of pine when you block.

label: Bell coat
desc: Jingles when you walk—festive and loud. Balances defense and nimbleness.

label: Parade plate
desc: Ceremonial plate in red and white, wool-lined—surprisingly tough.

label: Iceveil robe
desc: Woven with fine snow-thread; cool to the touch. Good against slashes.

label: Starfall aegis
desc: Meteor chips set in the breastplate; rumor says it softens a killing blow once.

label: Frostbite ring
desc: A frost pattern that never melts; steadies body and guard.

label: Mistletoe band
desc: Gifts exchanged beneath it are said to bring luck—helps your reflexes a little.

label: Coal ember loop
desc: Holds a warm coal charm—cozy on cold nights.

label: Yule log signet
desc: Seal shows burning holly—symbol that the long night ends.

label: Felt slippers
desc: Thick felt soles cushion trap stings—a kind choice for new adventurers.

label: Sleigh-runner boots
desc: Micro ice-ridges on the sole—grippy and good at shedding trap impact.

label: Aurora treaders
desc: Glow-thread in the shaft; legends say they glow softly when traps fire.

label: Starpath greaves
desc: Star-track runes on the shins—high boots that blunt spikes and pits.

label: Midnight silent boots
desc: Said to be worn on the longest night—quiet steps, traps find you less.

label: Hot cocoa flask (souvenir)
desc: You cannot really drink it, but it smells wonderful. Sellable trinket.

label: Snowglobe souvenir
desc: Shake it and the cottage snows. No combat bonus.

label: Reindeer badge
desc: Brass reindeer silhouette—said to guide lost travelers (mostly morale).

label: Elf whistle
desc: Does not whistle, but looks fine. A workshop reject.

label: Holly ink vial
desc: Bitter ink for copying wards—if you can write.

label: Comet shard
desc: A glittering ore splinter alchemists pay well for.

label: Ribbon whip
desc: Silk ribbon with silver bells—rings distract foes. Favors finesse.

label: Pine needle fan
desc: Pine ribs fan out like needles—great for nimble adventurers.
